title: NOC 41221 – Elementary School and Kindergarten Teachers | GTR Immigration
description: Elementary School and Kindergarten Teachers under NOC 41221 are licensed education professionals responsible for teaching young children foundational academic, social, and developmental skills across Canada. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role is essential to Canada’s public and private education systems. These educators shape early childhood learning experiences, build literacy and numeracy skills, and support children’s emotional and social development during their most formative years.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group412 – Professional occupations in education servicesMinor Group4122 – Secondary, elementary and kindergarten school teachersNOC Code41221

common_job_titles: English as a Second Language elementary school teacher
French as a Second Language elementary school teacher
duties: Elementary School and Kindergarten Teachers perform some or all of the following duties:
Prepare and deliver lessons according to approved curriculum
Teach through structured lesson plans, discussions, activities, and field trips
Promote physical, mental, and social development
Support school readiness and early literacy
Assess student progress and communicate with parents
Participate in staff meetings and professional development
Supervise teacher aides and student teachers
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in child development (may be required in some cases)
Additional training for:Special educationESL or French as a Second Language
ESL or French as a Second Language
Additional certification required for ESL or FSL instruction
Membership in provincial or territorial teachers’ association usually required
Foreign-trained teachers must complete credential assessment and obtain provincial certification before employment.
salary_prospects: Elementary and Kindergarten Teachers typically earn between:
$48,100 – $102,500 per year, depending on province, specialization, and years of experience.
Teachers in Northern territories may receive additional living allowances.
job_outlook: The employment outlook for NOC 41221 is strong and expanding due to:
Growth in early childhood education initiatives
Demand for bilingual and special education teachers
This occupation shows one of the highest projected growth figures within the education category.
labour_market_demand: Elementary and Kindergarten Teachers are employed in:
This represents one of the highest projected demands within the education sector.
Expansion of early childhood education programs
Growth in ESL and French immersion programs
Strong demand exists across Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, and Northern territories.
Strong demand in rural and northern communities
Clear career progression (Senior Teacher, Department Head, Vice Principal)
Teachers with specialization in French immersion, ESL, or special education are particularly in demand.
pathways: Provincial certification must be obtained before teaching.
